location.office_time_required: Monday, Tuesday, and Friday anchor days if within commuting distance of an office; fully remote otherwise

secondary_archetype: Program Manager with product strategy focus

level_signals: 8+ years in product operations, business operations, strategy, or closely adjacent function
Described as a 'senior, cross-functional role' and 'strategic leadership position'
Building and running a function from the ground up
Works in close partnership with CPO and EPD Leads
Base pay range $232,000–$348,000 in SF, consistent with Director-level

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years in product operations, business operations, strategy, or a closely adjacent function inside a high-growth technology company.
Ability to understand complex, technical products and articulate their value to non-technical audiences.
Deep familiarity with the full software development lifecycle — you understand how EPD teams plan, build, and ship, and have experience improving those processes.
Passion for structuring and solving highly ambiguous strategic problems and owning end-to-end execution of solutions.
Experience leading complex, cross-functional projects with multiple functional stakeholders.
Past experience working at the intersection of product and go-to-market teams in a software company.
Strong oral and written communication skills, with a proven ability to work effectively with multiple, cross-functional stakeholders, including users.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in developer tooling, cloud infrastructure, or platform products — you understand the Vercel customer and can speak credibly about the space.
Frequently build your own agents to support workflows and data analyses

scope.surface: Product Strategy & Operations function for the entire EPD (Engineering, Product, Design) organization at Vercel
primary_metrics_inferred: Team capacity and execution risk visibility
core_responsibilities: Own the EPD rhythm of business: weekly operating reviews, monthly business reviews, planning cycles, and roadmap processes — building minimum viable process for each.
Run the ShipOps function to enable Launch Calendar reviews and Vercel's high velocity 'You Can Just Ship Things' approach.

Establish a 'product landing' framework — a structured post-launch process that goes beyond the ship date to ensure new features and products are actually adopted, drive the intended business outcomes, and generate learnings that feed the next iteration.
Partner closely with Sales and Marketing teams to set targets, make plans and execute tactics that drive deal pipeline creation, and user launches and ramps.
Develop and maintain competitive intelligence systems that give product leaders a continuously updated view of the market landscape.
Partner with Data Engineering and Data Science to ensure the analytical infrastructure (data pipelines, warehouse models, tooling) supports the speed of analysis our business requires.
Partner with EPD leads to maintain team health, capacity visibility, and resource allocation so that the right bets are properly staffed and execution risk is surfaced early.

compensation.equity_form: equity-based compensation included in total package; form not specified

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed ($232K–$348K base in SF, competitive for the level)
High-visibility role reporting into CPO-level, building function from the ground up
Clear scope of ownership across three distinct areas (rhythm of business, measurement, planning)
Flexible remote eligibility for candidates outside commuting distance of an office
Company explicitly encourages applications from candidates who don't check every box
Vercel has strong brand recognition with notable customers (OpenAI, PayPal, Ramp)
red_flags: Role is described as building the function 'from the ground up' — high ambiguity and organizational complexity
Hybrid anchor days (Mon/Tue/Fri) for office-proximate candidates is a significant in-office commitment
Compensation outside SF may be adjusted downward — exact range for other locations not disclosed
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed in the JD
No mention of direct reports — unclear if this is an IC or people-manager role, though 'strategic leadership' language implies some management

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, 'senior' descriptor, 'strategic leadership position', and function-building mandate)
seniority.manages_people (inferred from 'building and running a function from the ground up' and 'strategic leadership position'; not explicitly confirmed)
scope.business_model (inferred from product descriptions and customer list)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated as KPIs in JD)
location.work_auth and location.visa_sponsorship (not mentioned in JD; marked unclear)
ambiguities: The JD does not clarify whether this role has direct reports or is a senior IC. 'Building and running the function from the ground up' and 'strategic leadership position' suggest people management is likely, but it is not confirmed.
Compensation range ($232K–$348K) applies to San Francisco only; the JD states 'Compensation outside of San Francisco may be adjusted based on employee location' without providing alternate ranges.
Remote eligibility depends on commuting distance to an office (SF, NY, London, Berlin) — the threshold distance is not defined; candidates must connect with recruiting for location-specific details.
